title: Partnering With Methadone Clinics “Transforms” Hep C Care, Study Finds
blurb: Reports on a study showing that offering hepatitis C treatment through opioid treatment programs can improve access for people who use opioids.

title: Why Is Autism More Common in Males?
blurb: Profiles research into how X chromosome genes may reduce female risk for autism and potentially protect against other developmental disorders.

title: Primary Care “Opioid Risk Scores” Poorly Predict Individual OD Risk
blurb: Reports on an external evaluation finding that an opioid risk score used in primary care poorly predicted individual overdose or opioid use disorder risk.

title: Campus Harm Reduction Fueled by Student Advocacy, Some State Policies
blurb: Looks at how student advocacy and state policies have expanded harm reduction resources on college campuses.

title: Discontinued Canadian Safe Supply Program Was Working, Study Shows
blurb: Reports on research suggesting that a discontinued Canadian safer supply program was producing positive outcomes.

title: Naloxone Boxes: New Strategy Makes OD Antidote Accessible 24/7, for Free
blurb: Covers naloxone box programs that make overdose reversal medication freely available in public spaces around the clock.

title: To Move Beyond Abstinence-Only, We Need Better Tools to Measure Recovery
blurb: Examines the need for more holistic, person-centered tools to measure recovery from substance use disorder beyond abstinence alone.

title: Fighting Breast Cancer in Rural America
blurb: Profiles research on the diversity of rural communities and how more precise approaches could improve breast cancer screening and public health interventions.

title: Drug Testing Defeats the Purpose of “Second Chance” Hiring
blurb: Explores how workplace drug testing can undermine second-chance hiring efforts for people with prior convictions.

title: Indigenous OD Mortality Plummets in Minnesota, But Disparity Remains
blurb: Reports on a decline in Indigenous overdose mortality in Minnesota while noting that major disparities persist.

title: Tennessee Harm Reductionist Faces Charges for Drug Checking
blurb: Covers the arrest of a Tennessee harm reductionist and the legal risks facing people involved in drug checking work.

title: People Seeking Methadone or Bupe Face “More Stress Than You Know”
blurb: Reports on the barriers and stress people face when trying to access methadone or buprenorphine treatment.

title: Harm Reduction Vending Machines Work, Says 30 Years of Research
blurb: Summarizes decades of research on harm reduction vending machines and their role in expanding access to supplies.

title: Music Festival Attendees Embrace DIY Drug Checking Despite Barriers
blurb: Explores how music festival attendees use do-it-yourself drug checking methods despite practical and legal barriers.

title: Test Strips Do Tell You If Xylazine Is Present, But Prone to False Positives
blurb: Reviews research on xylazine test strips, finding that they can detect xylazine but may also produce false positives.

title: Secret Shoppers Struggle to Buy Naloxone, Syringes at Texas Pharmacies
blurb: Reports on a study using secret shoppers to test access to over-the-counter naloxone and non-prescription syringes in Texas pharmacies.

title: Community “Bad Batch” Alerts Contrast With Algorithmic OD Prevention
blurb: Compares grassroots bad-batch alert systems with algorithmic overdose prevention approaches.

title: What’s in the Drug “Strips” Common in Prisons and Jails?
blurb: Explains the substances found in drug-soaked paper strips in prisons and jails and what research reveals about their risks.

title: In Rural Areas, People Who Use Drugs Face Particularly Severe Housing Crisis
blurb: Reports on research showing high rates of homelessness among rural people who use unregulated opioids or inject drugs.

title: BC Drug-Decrim Study Explores Problems With 2.5-Gram Threshold
blurb: Covers research on British Columbia’s drug decriminalization policy and concerns about its possession threshold.

title: Safer Drug Supply on the Dark Web? It’s Complicated, Researchers Find
blurb: Looks at research on whether buying drugs on the dark web may reduce some risks while introducing other complications.

title: Obituaries Experiment Highlights Stigma Against Overdose Victims
blurb: Examines how stigma affects the way overdose deaths are described and remembered in obituaries.

title: Aviation prodigy Cromwell Dixon's career took flight over Columbus
blurb: Traces the Columbus roots of aviation prodigy Cromwell Dixon, who became the first person to fly across the Continental Divide.
